headline: How The Blackstone (BX) Story Is Shifting With BCRED Pressures And Alternatives Growth Potential
summary: Blackstone's fair value estimate has been trimmed slightly to US$162.26 per share, down from US$166.72 per share, a small adjustment that still matters if you are watching the stock closely. Analysts are weighing pressure from BCRED redemptions and retail credit flows against the longer term potential of Blackstone's wealth and alternatives platform, which is feeding into these more cautious price targets. Read on to see what is driving the split views and how you can track the evolving...

headline: Is it Time to Sell Blackstone Stock Amid Rising Private Credit Woes?
summary: BX faces heavy redemptions in its $82B BCRED fund as private credit worries mount and shares drop 28% in three months. Is it time to rethink the stock?

headline: A Look At Blackstone’s (BX) Valuation As Private Credit Redemptions Draw Fresh Scrutiny
summary: Why Blackstone’s private credit redemptions matter for BX holders Blackstone (BX) is back in focus after record redemption requests hit its flagship private credit fund, prompting a US$400 million capital injection and a higher share repurchase cap to meet elevated withdrawal demand. See our latest analysis for Blackstone. Those private credit redemption headlines are landing at a time when Blackstone’s 30 day share price return of 14.68% and year to date share price return of 30.32% decline...

headline: Blackstone Balances Life Sciences Bet And REIT Interest As Valuation Lags
summary: Blackstone Life Sciences and Johnson & Johnson have agreed to co fund clinical trials of bleximenib, an investigational menin inhibitor for acute myeloid leukemia, marking their first joint research funding effort. Separately, Blackstone is reported to be exploring an acquisition of Whitestone REIT, which has engaged advisors and received confidentiality agreements from interested private equity firms. These moves arrive at a time when Blackstone, NYSE:BX, is drawing attention for activity...

headline: Why Blackstone and BlackRock Can Ride Out the Private-Credit Storm
summary: There may be little that  Blackstone  or  BlackRock  can do to stop the runaway train of negativity about private credit.  Last week, the two giant fund managers both reported that they faced jumps in first-quarter redemption requests from their flagship nontraded private-credit funds, which cater to wealthy individual investors.  What got much less attention, however, was that Blackstone’s flagship nontraded real-estate fund recently had its best month for investor flows in years.
